title: Create a Flask app with modular header and footer

content.problemStatement:
In this worked out example, we'll create a simple Flask application that demonstrates how to organize header and footer as separate template files. 
Follow these steps:

If you check the file structure on the right, you will find the following
```python
   my_flask_app/
   ├── main.py
   ├── templates/
   │   ├── base.html
   │   └── components/
   │       ├── header.html
   │       └── footer.html
```

### How does this code work?
When you run the Flask application - you will find that the Header and Footer has loaded on the webpage. 
- `base.html` includes the following code - `% include 'components/header.html' %` which loads `header.html` stored in the 'components' folder
- `base.html` includes the following code - `% include 'components/footer.html' %` which loads `footer.html` stored in the 'components' folder

**Note:** header.html, footer.html, navigation.html or any such components necessarily have to be stored in the 'components' folder within the 'templates' directory.
